title: The Library Cat
summary: A child meets the old cat that lives in the small village library and chats with the librarian about him.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Excuse me, Mr. Bruno. There is a cat on this chair!

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: Oh, that is Pico. He lives here in the library.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: A cat lives in the library? Really?

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: Yes, really. He came here ten years ago.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Can I touch him? He is very soft.

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: Of course. He likes children. But please be quiet.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: He is purring! He is so warm.

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: He sits on warm books. He likes the science shelf best.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Where does he sleep at night?

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: In a small basket near my desk. I close the door and he stays inside.

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: I give him fish in the morning and milk in the afternoon. Sometimes children bring him a small treat.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Does he have a favourite child?

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: He likes a quiet girl called Sofia. She reads to him every Friday.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Can I read to him too?

body.speaker: Mr. Bruno
body.text: Yes. Pick a small book and sit next to him. He will listen.

annotations.exampleEn: Excuse me, where is the toilet?
The kitten's fur is very soft.
Please be quiet in the museum.
The cat is purring on my lap.
The cooking books are on the top shelf.
The dog sleeps in a small basket.
Her desk is near the window.
The dog gets a treat after his walk.
Sofia is a quiet girl who loves books.
Pick a small book from the shelf.

grammarNotes.title: Polite requests with 'Can I…?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi muốn xin phép, ta dùng 'Can I + động từ + …?': 'Can I touch him?' / 'Can I read to him too?' Câu trả lời tích cực thường là 'Of course.' hoặc 'Yes.' Để đề nghị lịch sự ta thêm 'please'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To ask permission, use 'Can I + verb + …?': 'Can I touch him?' / 'Can I read to him too?' Positive replies are usually 'Of course.' or 'Yes.' Add 'please' to make a request more polite.

grammarNotes.title: Talking about a routine with 'in the morning / afternoon'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói khi nào việc xảy ra trong ngày, ta dùng 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'at night': 'I give him fish in the morning and milk in the afternoon.' / 'Where does he sleep at night?' Lưu ý: dùng 'at night' chứ không phải 'in the night'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To say when in the day something happens, use 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'at night': 'I give him fish in the morning and milk in the afternoon.' / 'Where does he sleep at night?' Note: we say 'at night', not 'in the night'.

translationVi: Bé: Cháu xin lỗi chú Bruno. Có một con mèo trên ghế này ạ! Chú Bruno: À, đó là Pico. Nó sống ở đây trong thư viện. Bé: Một con mèo sống trong thư viện ạ? Thật sao chú? Chú Bruno: Thật chứ. Nó đến đây từ mười năm trước. Bé: Cháu vuốt nó được không ạ? Nó mềm quá. Chú Bruno: Được chứ. Nó thích trẻ con. Nhưng cháu nhớ giữ yên lặng nhé. Bé: Nó đang kêu rừ rừ kìa! Nó ấm quá. Chú Bruno: Nó hay nằm trên những cuốn sách ấm. Nó thích kệ sách khoa học nhất. Bé: Ban đêm nó ngủ ở đâu ạ? Chú Bruno: Trong một cái giỏ nhỏ gần bàn của chú. Chú đóng cửa và nó ở trong này. Bé: Nó ăn gì ạ? Chú Bruno: Buổi sáng chú cho nó cá, buổi chiều cho nó sữa. Đôi khi bọn trẻ mang đến cho nó chút đồ ăn vặt. Bé: Nó có đứa nhỏ nào nó thích nhất không ạ? Chú Bruno: Nó thích một cô bé trầm tính tên là Sofia. Thứ Sáu nào cô bé cũng đọc sách cho nó nghe. Bé: Cháu đọc cho nó nghe được không ạ? Chú Bruno: Được. Cháu chọn một cuốn sách nhỏ rồi ngồi xuống cạnh nó. Nó sẽ lắng nghe.

questions.explanation: 'that is Pico. He lives here in the library.'
questions.hint: The child asks about an animal.
Look at Mr. Bruno's first answer.

questions.prompt: When did Pico come to the library?
What must the child do near the cat?
questions.hint: Find the rule in the library.

questions.prompt: Which shelf does Pico like best?
Where does Pico sleep at night?
options: In a basket near the desk
questions.explanation: 'In a small basket near my desk.'

questions.prompt: What does Pico eat in the morning?
questions.explanation: 'I give him fish in the morning.'

questions.prompt: What does Pico drink in the afternoon?

questions.explanation: 'He likes a quiet girl called Sofia.'

questions.prompt: What does Sofia do every Friday?
What does 'purring' mean in the dialogue?
options: A soft happy sound by a cat
questions.explanation: Pico is purring because he is warm and happy.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: A child learns about the library's cat
Sofia is sad about the cat
questions.explanation: The child asks many questions about Pico and gets to read to him.
questions.hint: Think about why they are talking.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some libraries keep a cat or a dog inside. Do you think it is a good idea? Why or why not?
